document.title: Clinical Evaluation Report for Digital Flat Panel X-ray Detector

sourceSummary.oneLine: Rev. 5 clinical evaluation report for H&abyz Digital Flat Panel X-ray Detector models A1717MCW, A1417MCW, and F1417MCW under EU MDR clinical evaluation requirements.
sourceSummary.officialUse: Supports clinical evaluation for safety, performance, benefit-risk, undesirable side-effects, equivalence, PMS, and PMCF under Regulation (EU) 2017/745 and MEDDEV 2.7/1 Rev. 4.
keyPoints: The device is undergoing initial CE-marking and is classified as class IIb under Rule 10 of Annex VIII Regulation (EU) 2017/745.
The intended purpose is general radiographic diagnosis of human anatomy for adults and children, excluding infants and pregnant women.
The report concludes conformity with MDR GSPR 1, GSPR 2, and GSPR 8.
Equivalent CE-marked devices named in the report are DRX-1, CXDI-70C, and CXDI-31.
PMS and PMCF updates report no adverse events or vigilance reports for 2025.

concepts.name: Clinical evaluation
concepts.evidence: This report is made in accordance with the MEDDEV 2.7/1 and Regulation (EU) 2017/745, Annex XIV.

concepts.name: General Safety and Performance Requirements
concepts.evidence: we conclude that the Digital Flat Panel X-ray Detector, is compliance to General Safety and Performance Requirements 1... General Safety and Performance Requirement 2... and General Safety and Performance Requirement 8

concepts.name: Medical device classification Rule 10
concepts.evidence: Therefore, this device is classified as class IIb in accordance with the rule 10 of Annex VIII Regulation (EU) 2017/745.

concepts.name: Demonstration of equivalence
concepts.evidence: We have demonstrated equivalence according to the Demonstration of Equivalence requirements of MEDDEV 2.7 / 1 Rev.4

concepts.name: Post-market surveillance
concepts.evidence: According to the PMS plan (A-PMS-5-001 Post-market Surveillance Plan), we will collect and utilize available information such as incident documentation, feedback and customer complaints

concepts.name: Post-market clinical follow-up
concepts.evidence: The PMCF will be conducted as a part of the PMS activities.

evidence.claim: The document is Rev. 5 of Clinical Evaluation Report A-TCF-5-118 dated December 30, 2025.
evidence.evidenceText: Technical Documentation Document No. A-TCF-5-118 Rev. No. 5 Clinical Evaluation Report Rev. Date December 30, 2025

evidence.claim: The report covers Digital Flat Panel X-ray Detector models A1717MCW, A1417MCW, and F1417MCW.
evidence.evidenceText: Product name: Digital Flat Panel X-ray Detector Model name: A1717MCW / A1417MCW / F1417MCW

evidence.claim: The report was prepared for EU MDR clinical evaluation requirements and MEDDEV 2.7/1.
evidence.evidenceText: This report is made in accordance with the MEDDEV 2.7/1 and Regulation (EU) 2017/745, Annex XIV.

evidence.claim: The device is undergoing initial CE-marking.
evidence.evidenceText: Whether the device is being developed/ undergoing initial CE-marking/ is CE-marked This device is undergoing initial CE-marking

evidence.claim: The device is classified as MDR class IIb under Rule 10.
evidence.evidenceText: Therefore, this device is classified as class IIb in accordance with the rule 10 of Annex VIII Regulation (EU) 2017/745.

evidence.claim: The intended purpose is general radiographic diagnosis of human anatomy for adults and children.
evidence.evidenceText: Digital Flat Panel X-ray Detector (model: A1717MCW / A1417MCW / F1417MCW) is indicated for digital imaging solution designed for providing general radiographic diagnosis of human anatomy targeting both adult and children.
evidence.pageTarget: Digital Flat Panel X-ray Detector Intended Purpose

evidence.claim: The device is not to be used for mammography, infants, pregnant women, fluoroscopy, or angiography.
evidence.evidenceText: Not to be used for mammography. Not to be used for infant and pregnant women. Not to be used for fluoroscopy and angiography.
evidence.pageTarget: Digital Flat Panel X-ray Detector Contraindications

evidence.claim: The report identifies DRX-1, CXDI-70C, and CXDI-31 as CE-marked equivalent devices.
evidence.evidenceText: DRX-1 of Carestream Health (CE marked by NB 0086); CXDI-70C of Canon (CE marked by NB 0197); CXDI-31 of Canon (CE marked by NB 0197)

evidence.claim: The clinical evaluation concludes the device complies with MDR GSPR 1, GSPR 2, and GSPR 8.
evidence.evidenceText: we conclude that the Digital Flat Panel X-ray Detector, is compliance to General Safety and Performance Requirements 1... General Safety and Performance Requirement 2... and General Safety and Performance Requirement 8

evidence.claim: The report states no known side effects for the device models.
evidence.evidenceText: The A1717MCW/A1417MCW/F1417MCW have no known side effects.
evidence.pageTarget: Digital Flat Panel X-ray Detector Side Effects

evidence.claim: The report states that 2025 PMS data showed no adverse events or vigilance reports.
evidence.evidenceText: the latest post-market data from 308 units sold in 2025 showed no adverse events or vigilance reports
evidence.pageTarget: Digital Flat Panel X-ray Detector PMS

evidence.claim: The clinical evaluation update frequency is every year.
evidence.evidenceText: The frequency of updates for the clinical evaluation report is every year.

reviewFlags.reason: Conflicting classification evidence: the classification checklist says the device is not intended for recording X-ray diagnostic images, but the same section classifies it as class IIb under Rule 10 for devices specifically intended for recording diagnostic images generated by X-ray radiation.
Conflicting technical evidence: device specification states A/D Conversion is 16 bits, while the equivalence table lists A/D Conversion for the device under clinical evaluation as 14 bits.
Customer feedback table lists 2025 Device complaints as 2, but the narrative says one customer complaint was recorded in 2025.
Some authority context is inferred from Regulation (EU) 2017/745 and CE-marking references; no notified body certificate for the subject device is provided in the extracted text.
